creator: jakub_lach@mailplus.pl
text:
Dnia 9 sierpnia 2011 8:13 Tilman KeskinÃ¶z <arved@FreeBSD.org> napisaÅ(a):

> On 08/08/2011 10:02 PM, Jakub Lach wrote:
> > 
> > Unfortunately, this is non-issue, because:
> > 
> > - development of ekg is in fact stalled, 
> > - they're not really interested in freebsd problems, 
> > (or maybe my rude emails weren't interesting enough
> > for them)
> > - maintainer of port no longer has ekg on FreeBSD, 
> > - patch didn't solve anything in the first place, 
> > newer version was unstable.
> > 
> > Maybe I wouldn't be so sarcastic, if they would 
> > release new ekg version since then, but no, apparently 
> > broken old client works good enough for them.
> > 
> > Sorry for taking your time.
> > 
> 
> If you can identify an earlier version that works, we can also downgrade
> the package.
> 
> Unfortunately i don't remember my gadugadu password :) and i don't speak
> polish so it is difficult for me to do this work...
> 
> If you think the program is completely useless, we can also remove it
> from the ports collection not to mislead other users.

Hello again.

Good news, I tested ekg-1.8rc2 and looks that it 
is usable. The contact list is displayed properly.

(usable by ekg standard, it still core dumped
on first run after saving settings, but this 
is fairly typical, and nothing new.)

Don't know about stability yet, and my system 
is substantially different now (8-STABLE-> 
9BETA1). 

The problem was, as described in PR, after
library bump, ekg couldn't display contact 
list, and new trunk version which could was 
unstable. 

So the main problem with ekg, is they release 
working RC 5+ months after substantial core 
library update, which prevented ekg from
working as it should. 

So I "vote" for bump to ekg-1.8rc2, at 
least it's working with new libgadu better
than current version in ports.

best regards, 
- Jakub Lach
